# mobench

> mobench is a Rust mobile benchmarking toolkit for Android and iOS. It provides a Cargo-native CLI, SDK runtime, `#[benchmark]` macros, generated mobile runners, BrowserStack App Automate integration, stable reports, schemas, and native profiling artifacts.

Use this file as a map for AI agents, coding assistants, and documentation readers. The canonical website is https://mobench.org and the canonical documentation site is https://docs.mobench.org.

## Primary Links

- Landing page: https://mobench.org
- Documentation root: https://docs.mobench.org/docs
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/worldcoin/mobile-bench-rs
- DeepWiki by Cognition: https://deepwiki.com/worldcoin/mobile-bench-rs
- DeepWiki overview: https://deepwiki.com/worldcoin/mobile-bench-rs/1-overview
- Crate docs: https://docs.rs/mobench/latest/mobench/
- SDK docs: https://docs.rs/mobench-sdk/latest/mobench_sdk/
- Macro docs: https://docs.rs/mobench-macros/latest/mobench_macros/

## What mobench is for

mobench was built to benchmark intense Rust workloads in mobile environments that represent real World App users. Real users run flagship phones, mid-tier devices, low-end devices, and older 32-bit devices. mobench helps teams measure whether critical Rust workloads, including cryptography used by World ID and ProveKit, perform reliably across that device range.

## Documentation Pages

- Overview: https://docs.mobench.org/docs
  High-level purpose, architecture, external AI documentation, and where to go next.
- Quickstart: https://docs.mobench.org/quickstart
  Install the CLI, register a benchmark, build mobile runners, and run locally or on BrowserStack.
- Installation: https://docs.mobench.org/install
  Rust, mobench, Android Studio, Android SDK and ADB, Xcode, iOS simulators, and BrowserStack credentials.
- Concepts: https://docs.mobench.org/concepts
  Benchmark definitions, generated runners, providers, report artifacts, schemas, and profiling outputs.
- Writing benchmarks: https://docs.mobench.org/authoring
  `#[benchmark]` functions, macro discovery, setup inputs, teardown, and benchmark authoring practices.
- Setup and teardown: https://docs.mobench.org/setup-teardown
  Untimed setup, measured iterations, teardown compatibility, and input lifetimes.
- SDK integration: https://docs.mobench.org/sdk
  `mobench-sdk`, runner backends, UniFFI, BoltFFI, native C ABI exports, and public runtime types.
- Build artifacts: https://docs.mobench.org/build
  Generated Android and iOS runner projects, runner backends, output paths, and verification commands.
- Local devices: https://docs.mobench.org/local-devices
  Connected Android devices, Android Studio emulators, ADB, Xcode simulators, and connected iOS devices.
- BrowserStack CI: https://docs.mobench.org/browserstack
  Hosted real-device runs, matrix resolution, split-sample merging, PR reporting, artifacts, resource metrics, and CI contracts.
- App Automate setup: https://docs.mobench.org/app-automate
  BrowserStack App Automate API uploads, Android Espresso, iOS XCUITest, build execution, devices, pricing, and trial limits.
- Profiling: https://docs.mobench.org/profiling
  Native profiling, trace events, semantic phases, flamegraphs, profile diffs, and flamegraph viewer usage.
- Outputs and reports: https://docs.mobench.org/reports
  Summary JSON, Markdown reports, CSV rows, split-run outputs, BrowserStack artifacts, PR comments, and profiling bundles.
- CLI man pages: https://docs.mobench.org/cli-reference
  Exhaustive command reference, global options, subcommands, flags, expected inputs, outputs, and examples.
- Schemas: https://docs.mobench.org/schemas
  CI metadata, summary JSON, trace events, fixture payloads, and machine-readable contracts.
- Examples explained: https://docs.mobench.org/examples
  Basic benchmark and native C ABI / FFI examples, plus how fixture outputs map to reports.
- Diagrams: https://docs.mobench.org/diagrams
  Crate architecture, lifecycle, provider flow, BrowserStack CI, profiling artifacts, and SDK versus CLI responsibility.
- Current behavior: https://docs.mobench.org/current-spec
  Current behavior and API contract for release 0.1.45.
- Codebase architecture: https://docs.mobench.org/codebase
  Workspace layout, runtime layers, generated templates, integrations, stack, and ownership boundaries.
- Testing strategy: https://docs.mobench.org/testing
  Host tests, CLI smoke checks, fixture validation, CI contracts, BrowserStack smoke tests, and profiling checks.
- Public API: https://docs.mobench.org/public-api
  Published crates, feature flags, serialized contracts, errors, semver policy, MSRV, and release readiness.
- Troubleshooting: https://docs.mobench.org/troubleshooting
  Common setup, build, discovery, BrowserStack, profiling, schema, and result-quality issues.

## Important External References

- Rust install: https://www.rust-lang.org/tools/install
- Android Studio: https://developer.android.com/studio
- Android SDK platform tools: https://developer.android.com/tools/releases/platform-tools
- Espresso: https://developer.android.com/training/testing/espresso
- Xcode: https://developer.apple.com/xcode/
- XCTest and XCUITest: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xctest
- BrowserStack: https://www.browserstack.com/
- BrowserStack App Automate: https://www.browserstack.com/app-automate
- BrowserStack App Automate API: https://www.browserstack.com/docs/app-automate/api-reference/introduction
- ProveKit: https://provekit.org/
- World ID: https://docs.world.org/world-id
- World App: http://world.org/world-app

## Common Commands

```bash
cargo install mobench
cargo mobench init
cargo mobench doctor
cargo mobench check --target android
cargo mobench build --target android --release
cargo mobench run --local --function fibonacci_30 --iterations 100 --warmup 10
cargo mobench ci run --target android --browserstack --output target/mobench/ci
cargo mobench ci merge-split-runs --samples-dir target/mobench/ci/split --output-dir target/mobench/ci/merged --function sample_fns::fibonacci --device "Google Pixel 7-13.0" --iterations 5
cargo mobench profile run --target android --function fibonacci_30 --output target/mobench/profile
```

## Output Artifacts

- `summary.json`: normalized benchmark summary.
- `summary.md`: human-readable run summary and PR comment source.
- `results.csv`: tabular sample data.
- `plots/`: optional rendered charts.
- `trace-events.json`: native profiling trace-event output.
- `profile-manifest.json`: profiling bundle index.
- Flamegraph bundles: interactive profiling artifacts consumed by the flamegraph viewer.

## Notes For Agents

- Prefer the docs pages above for authored explanations.
- Use DeepWiki when you need repository-indexed, source-linked context.
- Use docs.rs for exact Rust API surfaces.
- Use the CLI man pages page for exhaustive command flags and expected inputs or outputs.
- Use the schemas page when generating or validating automation that consumes mobench artifacts.
